Package de.uka.ilkd.key.macros

Class AbstractProofMacro

java.lang.Object
de.uka.ilkd.key.macros.AbstractProofMacro
All Implemented Interfaces:
ProofMacro
Direct Known Subclasses:
AbstractFinishAuxiliaryComputationMacro, AlternativeMacro, DoWhileFinallyMacro, ExhaustiveProofMacro, SequentialProofMacro, SkipMacro, StartAuxiliaryBlockComputationMacro, StartAuxiliaryLoopComputationMacro, StartAuxiliaryMethodComputationMacro, StrategyProofMacro, TryCloseMacro
public abstract class AbstractProofMacro extends Object implements ProofMacro
Takes care of providing the whole ProofMacro interface by only making it necessary to implement to most general application methods for a given list of goals and translating the less general versions (firstly for a given node and secondly having neither any goals nor a node). Although all these methods can be redefined by inheritance, this is usually not necessary, unless you know exactly what you are doing. The exception is #finishAfterMacro() for compound macros (see description in ProofMacro#finishAfterMacro()).

    • AbstractProofMacro

      public AbstractProofMacro()

  • Method Details

    • getScriptCommandName

      public String getScriptCommandName()
      Gets a unique short name for this macro that can be used in proof scripts. If null is returned, the macro cannot be addressed from within scripts. By default, proof macros do not support scripts, thus null is returned.
      Specified by:
      getScriptCommandName in interface ProofMacro
      Returns:
      null if not supported, or a non-null constant string as the short name

    • hasParameter

      public boolean hasParameter(String paramName)
      Description copied from interface: ProofMacro
      Checks whether this ProofMacro has a parameter named paramName. For use in proof scripts.
      Specified by:
      hasParameter in interface ProofMacro
      Parameters:
      paramName - The name to check.
      Returns:
      true iff this ProofMacro has a parameter named paramName.

    • setParameter

      public void setParameter(String paramName, String paramValue) throws IllegalArgumentException
      Description copied from interface: ProofMacro
      Sets the parameter named paramName to the given String representation in paramValue. For use in proof scripts.
      Specified by:
      setParameter in interface ProofMacro
      Parameters:
      paramName - The name of the parameter.
      paramValue - The value of the parameter.
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if there is no parameter of that name or the value is incorrectly formatted (e.g., cannot be converted to a number).

    • resetParams

      public void resetParams()
      Description copied from interface: ProofMacro
      Resets the macro parameters to their defaults.
      Specified by:
      resetParams in interface ProofMacro

    • canApplyTo

      public boolean canApplyTo(Node node, PosInOccurrence posInOcc)
      Description copied from interface: ProofMacro
      Can this macro be applied on the given node? This method should not make any changes but check if the macro can be applied or not on the given node. This method may be called from within the GUI thread and be compatible with that fact. This method must be implemented to have the same effect as calling ProofMacro.canApplyTo(Proof, ImmutableList, PosInOccurrence) with node.proof() as proof and all open goals below node.
      Specified by:
      canApplyTo in interface ProofMacro
      Parameters:
      node - the node (not null)
      posInOcc - the position in occurrence (may be null)
      Returns:
      true, if the macro is allowed to be applied

    • applyTo

      public ProofMacroFinishedInfo applyTo(UserInterfaceControl uic, Node node, PosInOccurrence posInOcc, ProverTaskListener listener) throws Exception
      Description copied from interface: ProofMacro
      Apply this macro on the given node. This method can change the proof by applying rules to it. This method is usually called from a dedicated thread and not the GUI thread. The thread it runs on may be interrupted. In this case, the macro may report the interruption by an InterruptedException. A ProverTaskListener can be provided to which the progress will be reported. If no reports are desired, null can be used for this parameter. If more than one listener is needed, consider combining them using a single listener object using the composite pattern.
      Specified by:
      applyTo in interface ProofMacro
      Parameters:
      uic - the UserInterfaceControl to use
      node - the node (not null)
      posInOcc - the position in occurrence (may be null)
      listener - the listener to use for progress reports (may be null)
      Throws:
      InterruptedException - if the application of the macro has been interrupted.
      Exception

    • getMaxSteps

      protected final int getMaxSteps(Proof proof)
      Gets the maximum number of rule applications allowed for a macro. The implementation is the maximum amount of proof steps for automatic mode.
      Returns:
      the maximum number of rule applications allowed for this macro
